problem.title: Excel Sheet Column Title
problem.description: Given an integer columnNumber, return its corresponding column title as it appears in an Excel sheet.
assumptions: Like base-26 but with A=1 instead of 0.

example.explanation: 28 = 1*26 + 2 → 'A'+'B' = 'AB'. It's base-26 but with no zero (A=1, ..., Z=26).

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Base 26 Conversion [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: This is the reverse of column number to title: convert an integer to a bijective base-26 string using letters A-Z. The key trick is to decrement columnNumber by 1 before taking the modulus so that 'Z' (26) maps correctly to index 25 rather than 0. Characters are prepended by repeatedly extracting the least significant 'digit'.
